images.caption: Figure 1:End-to-end workflow : lyric collection and metadata harmonization, filtering and balancing by artist gender and macro-region (ethnicity), translation of non-English lyrics into English, zero-shot author-profiling with multiple instruction-tuned LLMs under several prompt variants, and bias/performance analysis, complemented by self-explanation auditing.

Figure 2:Accuracy results for the different models with the different prompts type on the ethnicity-balanced dataset for the ethnicity, and on the gender ethnicity balanced dataset for the gender. Exact values and confidence interval available in the Appendix, Tab.6.
Figure 3:Gender prediction distribution on the gender-balanced dataset.
Figure 4:Ethnicity prediction distribution on the ethnicity-balanced dataset.
Figure 5:Roc curve points per modalities of gender and ethnicity for every LLM studied.
(a)Asia
(a)Asia
(b)Europe
(c)Oceania
(d)South America
Figure 7:Lyric length impact on the gender and ethnicity accuracy using the ethnicity balanced dataset.
Figure 8:Lyric length distribution given the gender.
Figure 9:Lyric length distribution given the ethnicity.
Figure 10:Lyric length impact on the gender and ethnicity accuracy using the ethnicity balanced dataset.
Figure 11:Translation impact on the gender predictions per model using the gender balanced dataset. We show range value for the different prompt results.
Figure 12:Translation impact on the ethnicity predictions per model using the ethnicity balanced dataset. We show range value for the different prompt results.

Figure 13:Translation impact on ethnicity prediction, highlighting the difference with and without North American artists using the ethnicity-balanced dataset. We show range value for the different prompt results.
Figure 14:Musical genre impact on ethnicity and gender prediction using the ethnicity balanced dataset.

Figure 15:P-values of the statistical tests per model and modality, evaluating the rejection of the hypothesisℋ0\mathcal{H}_{0}: predicted and balanced distribution are equivalent. The p-values are obtained through stratified bootstrapping. Models with a p-values under 0.05 p-value threshold reject this hypothesis and are considered biased.
Figure 16:Word clouds of the most frequently used words by DeepSeek-7B in its ethnicity classification explanations for Asia, Africa.
Figure 17:Word clouds of the most frequently used words by DeepSeek-7B in its ethnicity classification explanations for Oceania, North America and Latin America.
Figure 18:Word clouds of the most frequently used words by Llama 3.1 8B in its ethnicity classification explanations for Asia, Africa.
Figure 19:Word clouds of the most frequently used words by Llama 3.1 8B in its ethnicity classification explanations for Oceania, North America and Latin America.
